description:
The frontmatter body extraction has an off-by-one: when '
---
' is found at position end_pos in rest, the body is set to rest[end_pos + 5..] (feed.rs:364). The delimiter '
---
' is 5 bytes, so this skips the delimiter correctly. However for the '
---
' case (Windows line endings), the code OR-chains with find('
---
') but still advances by +5, which would leave '
' at the start of the body. This is an untested edge case. Additionally, the fallback branch content.starts_with('---
') at line 360 is handled but the end detection or_else chain at line 362 does not handle '
---
' — the mixed CRLF terminator case. No test covers CRLF frontmatter. REQ-FEED-022 specifies extracting frontmatter from '.md' files which may have CRLF line endings on Windows targets.

evidence.snippet:
if content.starts_with("---
") || content.starts_with("---
") {
    let rest = &content[4..];
    if let Some(end_pos) = rest.find("
---
").or_else(|| rest.find("
---
")) {
        let fm = &rest[..end_pos];
        body = rest[end_pos + 5..].to_string(); // skips '
---
' but wrong for '
---
' (6 bytes)

crossReference: specs/behavioral-spec.md REQ-FEED-022 — requires scanning vault .md files (platform-agnostic)
